chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ جَاوَزَ مِيقَاتَ أَرْضِهِ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ أَوْ دَخَلَ مَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 76 – The one who exceeds the land of a juncture without Ihraam, or enters Makkah without Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/247)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/247)
text: مَنْ كَانَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ عَلَى مَسِيرَةِ عَشَرَةِ أَمْيَالٍ لَمْ يَدْخُلْهَا إِلَّا بِإِحْرَامٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from Ahmad Bin Amro Bin Saeed, from Wardaan,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassanasws the 1st having said: ‘The one who was away from Makkah upon a travel distance of ten miles cannot enter it (the Harram) except in Ihraam’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from Ahmad ibn ‘Amr ibn Sa‘id from Wardan who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Whoever lives at a distance of ten miles from Makkah must not enter Makkah without Hiram.’”
